title: Area of Trapezoids
contentObjective: I can find the area of a trapezoid using the formula A = ½(b1 + b2) × h.
languageObjective: I can explain my steps using the words trapezoid, base, height, and area.

turnAndTalk.question: The trapezoidal window has a top edge of 4 feet and a bottom edge of 8 feet. Why does the area formula use BOTH bases instead of just one?
turnAndTalk.kernel: We use both bases because the top and bottom are different lengths.

stems.en: We use both bases because the top and bottom are ___.
stems.es: Usamos las dos bases porque la parte de arriba y la de abajo son ___.

stems.en: If I used only one base, the area would be ___.
stems.es: Si usara solo una base, el área sería ___.

turnAndTalk.listenFor: Students explain a trapezoid has two parallel sides of different lengths, so using only one would over- or under-count the area; strong answers mention averaging the bases.
turnAndTalk.extend: The wonder prompt asks what happens if both bases are equal. Predict the shape and explain how the trapezoid formula would simplify in that case.
extendStems: If b1 equals b2, the shape becomes a ___ because ___.

turnAndTalk.question: On the grid you copied the trapezoid (bases 8 and 4, height 5), flipped it, and joined it to the original. What shape formed, and how does that explain the 1/2 in the formula?
turnAndTalk.kernel: Two trapezoids make a parallelogram, so one trapezoid is half of it.

stems.en: When I flip and join the trapezoid, I make a ___.
stems.es: Cuando volteo y uno el trapecio, formo un ___.

stems.en: I added the two bases first because ___.
stems.es: Primero sumé las dos bases porque ___.

stems.en: The area is 1/2 times (8 plus 4) times 5, which equals ___.
stems.es: El área es 1/2 por (8 más 4) por 5, que es ___.

turnAndTalk.listenFor: A strong answer names a parallelogram, links the 1/2 to taking half of two combined trapezoids, and computes area = 1/2 x 12 x 5 = 30 sq ft.
turnAndTalk.extend: Compare finding a trapezoid's area to averaging the two bases and multiplying by the height. Are these the same method? Justify with the window numbers.
extendStems: Averaging the bases gives ___, and multiplying by the height gives ___, which ___ the formula answer.

turnAndTalk.question: The builder pours concrete for a trapezoidal driveway with edges of 20 ft and 12 ft and a height of 15 ft; one bag covers 6 sq ft. Walk through finding the number of bags.
turnAndTalk.kernel: Find the trapezoid area first, then divide by what one bag covers.

stems.en: The driveway area is 1/2 times (20 plus 12) times 15, which equals ___ square feet.
stems.es: El área de la entrada es 1/2 por (20 más 12) por 15, que es ___ pies cuadrados.

stems.en: He needs ___ bags because ___ divided by 6 equals ___.

turnAndTalk.listenFor: Students compute area = 240 sq ft, then 240 / 6 = 40 bags, and explain why dividing by coverage gives the bag count.
turnAndTalk.extend: If the builder doubled only the height of the driveway, would the number of bags double? Justify using the formula.
extendStems: Doubling the height ___ the area because ___.
So the number of bags would ___, since ___.

vocabulary.definition: A four-sided shape with just one pair of parallel sides.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Una figura de cuatro lados con solo un par de lados paralelos.
vocabulary.visual: A table top wider than the bottom shelf — the top and bottom edges are parallel, the two sides slant inward
vocabulary.cloze: A quadrilateral with exactly one pair of parallel sides is a ___.

examples.text: A four-sided shape with one pair of parallel sides
examples.why: It has exactly one pair of parallel sides.
A rectangle has two pairs of parallel sides.

vocabulary.definition: One of the two parallel sides of a trapezoid.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Uno de los dos lados paralelos de un trapecio.
vocabulary.visual: If the bottom of the trapezoid is 10 ft, then b₁ = 10 ft in the formula A = ½(b₁ + b₂) × h
vocabulary.cloze: One of the two parallel sides of a trapezoid is ___.

vocabulary.definition: The other parallel side of a trapezoid.
vocabulary.definitionEs: El otro lado paralelo de un trapecio.
vocabulary.visual: If the top of the trapezoid is 6 ft, then b₂ = 6 ft; both bases are parallel to each other
vocabulary.cloze: The other parallel side of a trapezoid is ___.

vocabulary.definition: The straight-up distance between the two parallel sides.
vocabulary.definitionEs: La distancia recta entre los dos lados paralelos.
vocabulary.visual: A dashed vertical line from the top base straight down to the bottom base at a 90° angle — NOT the slanted side
vocabulary.cloze: The perpendicular distance between the two parallel bases is the ___.

vocabulary.definition: How much space is inside a flat shape.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Cuánto espacio hay dentro de una figura plana.
vocabulary.visual: A trapezoid with b₁ = 10, b₂ = 6, h = 4 has area = ½(10 + 6) × 4 = 32 sq units
vocabulary.cloze: The amount of flat space inside the trapezoid is its ___.

launch.narrative: Your architecture firm is designing a trapezoidal window for a modern building. The glass supplier charges by the square foot, so the team must calculate the exact area. The window has a top edge of 4 feet, a bottom edge of 8 feet, and a height of 5 feet.
launch.contextImage: Architectural rendering of a building facade with a trapezoidal window highlighted in blue
noticePrompts: How many parallel sides does it have?
wonderPrompts: Why does the formula use two bases instead of one?
What would happen if both bases were the same length?

conceptIntro.heading: How do we find the area of a trapezoid?
conceptIntro.intro: A trapezoid is a four-sided shape with one pair of parallel sides. The two parallel sides are called the bases, and they have different lengths.
conceptIntro.keyIdea: Area of a trapezoid = ½ × (base 1 + base 2) × height. We add the two bases first, then take half.

iDo.title: Watch me
lines: My trapezoid window has a top base of 4 feet, a bottom base of 8 feet, and a height of 5 feet.
I write the formula: A = ½ × (b1 + b2) × h.
I take half: ½ × 60 = 30. So the area is 30 square feet.

weDo.title: Let's try together
lines: Now a smaller trapezoid: bases 3 cm and 5 cm, height 2 cm.
Now take half: ½ × 16 = 8, so the area is 8 square centimeters.

youDo.title: Now it's your turn
lines: Next you will plot the window on a grid and find its area using the formula.
Remember the order: add the bases, multiply by the height, then take half.

explore.instructions: Plot the vertices of a trapezoid with bases of 8 and 4, and height of 5. Place points at (0, 0), (8, 0), (6, 5), and (2, 5).
explore.label: Plot the four vertices of the trapezoidal window.

discourse.prompt: If you copied this trapezoid, flipped it, and placed it next to the original, what shape would you get? How does this help you find the area?
discourse.sentenceFrame: The area of the trapezoid is 1/2 x (___ + ___) x ___ = 1/2 x ___ x ___ = ___ square feet.

optional.stem: Blueprint 1: A trapezoidal lobby window has bases of 6 ft and 10 ft and a height of 4 ft. What is its area for the glass order?
Blueprint 2: A trapezoidal skylight has bases of 9 ft and 7 ft and a height of 6 ft. What is its area?

optional.instructions: Each blueprint window lists its bases and height. Find each area, then sort the windows by whether they need more than 40 sq ft of glass.

categories.label: More than 40 sq ft of glass
40 sq ft of glass or less

optional.label: Match each blueprint window to the area of glass it needs.

optional.stem: Level 2 Extension: Two copies of a trapezoidal window (bases 5 ft and 11 ft) are flipped and joined to make a parallelogram with height 6 ft. The parallelogram's base is 5 + 11 = 16 ft, so its area is 96 sq ft. What is the area of ONE trapezoid window?
optional.explanation: One trapezoid is half the parallelogram: 96 ÷ 2 = 48 sq ft. This matches A = ½ × (5 + 11) × 6 = ½ × 16 × 6 = 48 sq ft — the ½ comes from the trapezoid being half of the parallelogram.

optional.title: Level 2 Extension — The Missing Half

workedExample.label: Read the blueprint
workedExample.work: Trapezoid window: b1 = 6 ft, b2 = 10 ft, h = 4 ft

workedExample.label: Add the bases, then multiply by height
State the area for the glass order

optional.correctWork: The student forgot to multiply by ½, so they ordered double the glass. The correct area is A = ½ × (6 + 10) × 4 = ½ × 64 = 32 sq ft, not 64 sq ft.
hints: Compare the final step to the formula — what part of ½ × (b1 + b2) × h is missing?
The student found the parallelogram area (64), but a trapezoid is only half of that, so take ½ of 64.

approaching.stem: What is the area of a trapezoid with bases 6 cm and 10 cm, and height 4 cm?
A trapezoid has an area of 45 sq ft, bases of 7 ft and 11 ft. What is the height?
What is the first step when finding the area of a trapezoid?
choices: Subtract the shorter base from the longer base
approaching.explanation: The formula is A = ½ × (b₁ + b₂) × h. The first step is to add the two parallel bases.

approaching.stem: A trapezoid has bases of 5 in and 9 in, and a height of 6 in. What is its area?

approaching.instructions: Use the formula A = ½ × (b₁ + b₂) × h to find the area of each trapezoid.

onLevel.label: Match each shape to its area.

onLevel.instructions: Calculate the area of each trapezoid, then sort by whether the area is greater than 40 sq units or not.
A trapezoid has bases of 6 ft and 10 ft, and an area of 48 sq ft. Place a point at the height. (Hint: 48 = ½ × 16 × h → h = 6)
onLevel.label: Find the missing height: b₁=6, b₂=10, A=48

extending.title: Find the Area Error

workedExample.label: Identify the bases and height
workedExample.work: b₁ = 10 m, b₂ = 6 m, h = 7 m

extending.correctWork: The student forgot to multiply by ½. A = ½ × 16 × 7 = ½ × 112 = 56 sq m.
hints: Look at Step 4. What happened to the ½?
The student skipped the ½ and just multiplied (b₁ + b₂) × h.

extending.prompt: A trapezoid and a parallelogram both have a height of 8 ft. The trapezoid has bases of 5 ft and 11 ft. The parallelogram has a base of 8 ft. Which shape has the greater area? Explain your reasoning using the formulas.
extending.sentenceFrame: The trapezoid's area is ___ because ½ × (___ + ___) × ___ = ___. The parallelogram's area is ___ because ___ × ___ = ___. The ___ has a greater area by ___ sq ft.

extending.instructions: Plot a trapezoid with b₁ = 10, b₂ = 4, and height = 6. Then calculate its area using the formula.
extending.label: Plot the trapezoid and calculate its area.

optionalActivity.name: Trapezoid Blueprint Challenge
optionalActivity.intro: Our architecture firm just landed three trapezoid-window blueprints, so let's calculate every area before the glass order goes out.

connect.scenario: A builder is pouring concrete for a trapezoidal driveway. The driveway has parallel edges of 20 feet and 12 feet with a height of 15 feet. One bag of concrete covers 6 square feet.
connect.promptQuestion: How many bags of concrete does the builder need?
connect.prompt: The driveway's area is ___ sq ft because A = 1/2 x (___ + ___) x ___. He needs ___ bags because ___ / 6 = ___.

exitTicket.stem: A trapezoid has bases of 9 inches and 5 inches, and a height of 6 inches. What is its area?
exitTicket.explanation: A = 1/2 x (b1 + b2) x h = 1/2 x (9 + 5) x 6 = 1/2 x 14 x 6 = 42 square inches.

projects.title: Unit 5 Project — Area in Action
projects.desc: Capstone: apply area of polygons and composite figures to a real design challenge.

projects.title: Area Architect — City Builder
projects.desc: Build a city by calculating areas of parallelograms, triangles, trapezoids, and composite figures.

projects.title: Graphic Novel #1 — Theme Park Engineer
projects.desc: Story-driven area mission across parallelograms, triangles, and trapezoids.
